2228	nbinomLogLike <- function(counts, mu, disp, weights, useWeights) {

2232	                           log=TRUE),ncol=ncol(counts)))

2235	                           log=TRUE),ncol=ncol(counts)))    
2236	  }
2237	}

2353	calculateCooksDistance <- function(object, H, modelMatrix) {
2354	  p <- ncol(modelMatrix)
2355	  dispersions <- robustMethodOfMomentsDisp(object, modelMatrix)
2356	  V <- assays(object)[["mu"]] + dispersions * assays(object)[["mu"]]^2
2357	  PearsonResSq <- (counts(object) - assays(object)[["mu"]])^2 / V
2358	  cooks <- PearsonResSq / p  * H / (1 - H)^2
2359	  cooks
2360	}

```r
788	# c' beta / sqrt( c' sigma c)
789	# where beta is the coefficient vector
790	# and sigma is the covariance matrix for beta
791	getContrast <- function(object, contrast, useT=FALSE, minmu) {
792	  if (missing(contrast)) {
793	    stop("must provide a contrast")
794	  }
795	  if (is.null(attr(object,"modelMatrix"))) {
796	    stop("was expecting a model matrix stored as an attribute of the DESeqDataSet")
797	  }
798	  modelMatrix <- attr(object, "modelMatrix")
799	  
800	  # only continue on the rows with non-zero row mean
801	  objectNZ <- object[!mcols(object)$allZero,]
802	  normalizationFactors <- getSizeOrNormFactors(objectNZ)
803	  alpha_hat <- dispersions(objectNZ)
804	  coefColumns <- names(mcols(objectNZ))[grep("log2 fold change",mcols(mcols(object))$description)]
805	  # convert betas to log scale
806	  beta_mat <- log(2) * as.matrix(mcols(objectNZ)[,coefColumns,drop=FALSE])
807	  # convert beta prior variance to log scale
808	  lambda = 1/(log(2)^2 * attr(object,"betaPriorVar"))
809	
810	  # check if DESeq() replaced outliers
811	  countsMatrix <- if ("replaceCounts" %in% assayNames(object)) {
812	    assays(objectNZ)[["replaceCounts"]]
813	  } else {
814	    counts(objectNZ)
815	  }
816	
817	  # use weights if they are present in assays(object)
818	  if ("weights" %in% assayNames(object)) {
819	    useWeights <- TRUE
820	    weights <- assays(objectNZ)[["weights"]]
821	    stopifnot(all(weights >= 0))
822	    weights <- weights / apply(weights, 1, max)
823	  } else {
824	    useWeights <- FALSE
825	    weights <- matrix(1, nrow=nrow(object), ncol=ncol(object))
826	  }
827	  
828	  betaRes <- fitBeta(ySEXP = countsMatrix, xSEXP = modelMatrix,
829	                     nfSEXP = normalizationFactors,
830	                     alpha_hatSEXP = alpha_hat,
831	                     contrastSEXP = contrast,
832	                     beta_matSEXP = beta_mat,
833	                     lambdaSEXP = lambda,
834	                     weightsSEXP = weights,
835	                     useWeightsSEXP = useWeights,
836	                     tolSEXP = 1e-8, maxitSEXP = 0,
837	                     useQRSEXP=FALSE, # QR not relevant, fitting loop isn't entered
838	                     minmuSEXP=minmu)
839	  # convert back to log2 scale
840	  contrastEstimate <- log2(exp(1)) * betaRes$contrast_num
841	  contrastSE <- log2(exp(1)) * betaRes$contrast_denom
842	  contrastStatistic <- contrastEstimate / contrastSE
843	  if (useT) {
844	    stopifnot("tDegreesFreedom" %in% names(mcols(object)))
845	    df <- mcols(objectNZ)$tDegreesFreedom
846	    contrastPvalue <- 2*pt(abs(contrastStatistic),df=df,lower.tail=FALSE)
847	  } else {
848	    contrastPvalue <- 2*pnorm(abs(contrastStatistic),lower.tail=FALSE)
849	  }
850	  contrastList <- list(log2FoldChange=contrastEstimate,
851	                       lfcSE=contrastSE,
852	                       stat=contrastStatistic,
853	                       pvalue=contrastPvalue)
854	  contrastResults <- buildDataFrameWithNARows(contrastList,
855	                                              mcols(object)$allZero)
856	  names(contrastResults) <- c("log2FoldChange","lfcSE","stat","pvalue")
857	  contrastResults
858	}
859	
860	# this function takes a desired contrast as specified by results(),
861	# performs checks, and then either returns the already existing contrast
862	# or generates the contrast by calling getContrast() using a numeric vector
863	cleanContrast <- function(object, contrast, expanded=FALSE, listValues, test, useT, minmu) {
864	  # get the names of columns in the beta matrix
865	  resNames <- resultsNames(object)
866	  # if possible, return pre-computed columns, which are
867	  # already stored in mcols(dds). this will be the case using
868	  # results() with 'name', or if expanded model matrices were not
869	  # run and the contrast contains the reference level as numerator or denominator
870	
871	  resReady <- FALSE
872	  
873	  if (is.character(contrast)) {
874	    contrastFactor <- contrast[1]
875	    if (!contrastFactor %in% names(colData(object))) {
876	      stop(paste(contrastFactor,"should be the name of a factor in the colData of the DESeqDataSet"))
877	    }
878	    if (!is(colData(object)[[contrastFactor]], "factor")) {
879	      stop(paste(contrastFactor,"is not a factor, see ?results"))
880	    }
881	    contrastNumLevel <- contrast[2]
882	    contrastDenomLevel <- contrast[3]
883	    contrastBaseLevel <- levels(colData(object)[,contrastFactor])[1]
884	    
885	    # check for intercept
886	    hasIntercept <- attr(terms(design(object)),"intercept") == 1
887	    firstVar <- contrastFactor == all.vars(design(object))[1]
888	
889	    # tricky case: if the design has no intercept, the factor is
890	    # not the first variable in the design, and one of the numerator or denominator
891	    # is the reference level, then the desired contrast is simply a coefficient (or -1 times)
892	    noInterceptPullCoef <- !hasIntercept & !firstVar &
893	      (contrastBaseLevel %in% c(contrastNumLevel, contrastDenomLevel))
894	    
895	    # case 1: standard model matrices: pull coef or build the appropriate contrast
896	    # coefficients names are of the form  "factor_level_vs_baselevel"
897	    # output: contrastNumColumn and contrastDenomColumn
898	    if (!expanded & (hasIntercept | noInterceptPullCoef)) {
899	      # use make.names() so the column names are
900	      # the same as created by DataFrame in mcols(object).
901	      contrastNumColumn <- make.names(paste0(contrastFactor,"_",contrastNumLevel,"_vs_",contrastBaseLevel))
902	      contrastDenomColumn <- make.names(paste0(contrastFactor,"_",contrastDenomLevel,"_vs_",contrastBaseLevel))
903	      # check that the desired contrast is already
904	      # available in mcols(object), and then we can either
905	      # take it directly or multiply the log fold
906	      # changes and Wald stat by -1
907	      if ( contrastDenomLevel == contrastBaseLevel ) {
908	        cleanName <- paste(contrastFactor,contrastNumLevel,"vs",contrastDenomLevel)
909	        # the results can be pulled directly from mcols(object)
910	        name <- if (!noInterceptPullCoef) {
911	          make.names(paste0(contrastFactor,"_",contrastNumLevel,"_vs_",contrastDenomLevel))
912	        } else {
913	          make.names(paste0(contrastFactor,contrastNumLevel))
914	        }
915	        if (!name %in% resNames) {
916	          stop(paste("as",contrastDenomLevel,"is the reference level, was expecting",name,"to be present in 'resultsNames(object)'"))
917	        }
918	        log2FoldChange <- getCoef(object, name)
919	        lfcSE <- getCoefSE(object, name)
920	        stat <- getStat(object, test, name)
921	        pvalue <- getPvalue(object, test, name)
922	        res <- cbind(mcols(object)["baseMean"],log2FoldChange,lfcSE,stat,pvalue)
923	        names(res) <- c("baseMean","log2FoldChange","lfcSE","stat","pvalue")
924	        lfcType <- if (attr(object,"betaPrior")) "MAP" else "MLE"
925	        lfcDesc <- paste0("log2 fold change (",lfcType,"): ",cleanName)
926	        mcols(res,use.names=TRUE)["log2FoldChange","description"] <- lfcDesc
927	        resReady <- TRUE
928	        
929	      } else if ( contrastNumLevel == contrastBaseLevel ) {
930	        # fetch the results for denom vs num 
931	        # and mutiply the log fold change and stat by -1
932	        cleanName <- paste(contrastFactor,contrastNumLevel,"vs",contrastDenomLevel)
933	        swapName <- if (!noInterceptPullCoef) {
934	          make.names(paste0(contrastFactor,"_",contrastDenomLevel,"_vs_",contrastNumLevel))
935	        } else {
936	          make.names(paste0(contrastFactor,contrastDenomLevel))
937	        }
938	        if (!swapName %in% resNames) {
939	          stop(paste("as",contrastNumLevel,"is the reference level, was expecting",swapName,"to be present in 'resultsNames(object)'"))
940	        }
941	        log2FoldChange <- getCoef(object, swapName)
942	        lfcSE <- getCoefSE(object, swapName)
943	        stat <- getStat(object, test, swapName)
944	        pvalue <- getPvalue(object, test, swapName)
945	        res <- cbind(mcols(object)["baseMean"],log2FoldChange,lfcSE,stat,pvalue)
946	        names(res) <- c("baseMean","log2FoldChange","lfcSE","stat","pvalue")
947	        res$log2FoldChange <- -1 * res$log2FoldChange
948	        if (test == "Wald") res$stat <- -1 * res$stat
949	        lfcType <- if (attr(object,"betaPrior")) "MAP" else "MLE"
950	        # rename some of the columns using the flipped contrast
951	        if (test == "Wald") {
952	          contrastDescriptions <- paste(c(paste0("log2 fold change (",lfcType,"):"),
953	                                          "standard error:",
954	                                          "Wald statistic:","Wald test p-value:"), cleanName)
955	          mcols(res,use.names=TRUE)[c("log2FoldChange","lfcSE","stat","pvalue"),
956	                      "description"] <- contrastDescriptions
957	        } else {
958	          contrastDescriptions <- paste(c(paste0("log2 fold change (",lfcType,"):"),
959	                                          "standard error:"), cleanName)
960	          mcols(res,use.names=TRUE)[c("log2FoldChange","lfcSE"),
961	                      "description"] <- contrastDescriptions
962	        }
963	        resReady <- TRUE
964	        
965	      } else {
966	        # check for the case where neither are present
967	        # as comparisons against reference level
968	        if ( ! (contrastNumColumn %in% resNames &
969	                  contrastDenomColumn %in% resNames) ) {
970	          stop(paste(contrastNumLevel,"and",contrastDenomLevel,"should be levels of",contrastFactor,"such that",contrastNumColumn,"and",contrastDenomColumn,"are contained in 'resultsNames(object)'"))
971	        }
972	      }
973	      # case 2: expanded model matrices or no intercept and first variable
974	      # need to then build the appropriate contrast.
975	      # these coefficient names have the form "factorlevel"
976	      # output: contrastNumColumn and contrastDenomColumn
977	    } else {
978	      # we only need to check validity
979	      contrastNumColumn <- make.names(paste0(contrastFactor, contrastNumLevel))
980	      contrastDenomColumn <- make.names(paste0(contrastFactor, contrastDenomLevel))
981	      if ( ! (contrastNumColumn %in% resNames & contrastDenomColumn %in% resNames) ) {
982	        stop(paste(paste0(contrastFactor,contrastNumLevel),"and",paste0(contrastFactor,contrastDenomLevel),
983	                   "are expected to be in resultsNames(object)"))
984	      }
985	    }
986	
987	    # check if both levels have all zero counts
988	    # (this has to be down here to make use of error checking above)
989	    contrastAllZero <- contrastAllZeroCharacter(object, contrastFactor,
990	                                                contrastNumLevel, contrastDenomLevel)
991	    
992	  }
993	
994	  # if the result table not already built in the above code
995	  if (!resReady) {
996	    
997	    # here, a numeric / list / character contrast which will be converted
998	    # into a numeric contrast and run through getContrast()
999	    if (is.numeric(contrast)) {
1000	      # make name for numeric contrast
1001	      signMap <- c("","","+")
1002	      contrastSigns <- signMap[sign(contrast)+2]
1003	      contrastName <- paste(paste0(contrastSigns,as.character(contrast)),collapse=",")
1004	    } else if (is.list(contrast)) {
1005	      # interpret list contrast into numeric and make a name for the contrast
1006	      lc1 <- length(contrast[[1]])
1007	      lc2 <- length(contrast[[2]])
1008	      # these just used for naming
1009	      listvalname1 <- round(listValues[1],3)
1010	      listvalname2 <- round(listValues[2],3)
1011	      if (lc1 > 0 & lc2 > 0) {
1012	        listvalname2 <- abs(listvalname2)
1013	        listvalname1 <- if (listvalname1 == 1) "" else paste0(listvalname1," ")
1014	        listvalname2 <- if (listvalname2 == 1) "" else paste0(listvalname2," ")
1015	        contrastName <- paste0(listvalname1,paste(contrast[[1]],collapse="+")," vs ",listvalname2,paste(contrast[[2]],collapse="+"))
1016	      } else if (lc1 > 0 & lc2 == 0) {
1017	        listvalname1 <- if (listvalname1 == 1) "" else paste0(listvalname1," ")
1018	        contrastName <- paste0(listvalname1,paste(contrast[[1]],collapse="+")," effect")
1019	      } else if (lc1 == 0 & lc2 > 0) {
1020	        contrastName <- paste(listvalname2,paste(contrast[[2]],collapse="+"),"effect")
1021	      }
1022	      contrastNumeric <- rep(0,length(resNames))
1023	      contrastNumeric[resNames %in% contrast[[1]]] <- listValues[1]
1024	      contrastNumeric[resNames %in% contrast[[2]]] <- listValues[2]
1025	      contrast <- contrastNumeric
1026	    } else if (is.character(contrast)) {
1027	      # interpret character contrast into numeric and make a name for the contrast
1028	      contrastNumeric <- rep(0,length(resNames))
1029	      contrastNumeric[resNames == contrastNumColumn] <- 1
1030	      contrastNumeric[resNames == contrastDenomColumn] <- -1
1031	      contrast <- contrastNumeric
1032	      contrastName <- paste(contrastFactor,contrastNumLevel,"vs",contrastDenomLevel)
1033	    }
1034	
1035	    contrastAllZero <- contrastAllZeroNumeric(object, contrast)
1036	
1037	    # now get the contrast
1038	    contrastResults <- getContrast(object, contrast, useT=useT, minmu)
1039	    lfcType <- if (attr(object,"betaPrior")) "MAP" else "MLE"
1040	    contrastDescriptions <- paste(c(paste0("log2 fold change (",lfcType,"):"),
1041	                                    "standard error:",
1042	                                    "Wald statistic:",
1043	                                    "Wald test p-value:"),
1044	                                  contrastName)
1045	    mcols(contrastResults) <- DataFrame(type=rep("results",ncol(contrastResults)),
1046	                                        description=contrastDescriptions)
1047	    res <- cbind(mcols(object)["baseMean"],
1048	                 contrastResults)
1049	
1050	  }
1051	    
1052	  # if the counts in all samples included in contrast are zero
1053	  # then zero out the LFC, Wald stat and p-value set to 1
1054	  contrastAllZero <- contrastAllZero & !mcols(object)$allZero
1055	  if (sum(contrastAllZero) > 0) {
1056	    res$log2FoldChange[contrastAllZero] <- 0
1057	    res$stat[contrastAllZero] <- 0
1058	    res$pvalue[contrastAllZero] <- 1
1059	  }
1060	
1061	  # if test is "LRT", overwrite the statistic and p-value
1062	  # (we only ran contrast for the coefficient)
1063	  if (test == "LRT") {
1064	    stat <- getStat(object, test, name=NULL)
1065	    pvalue <- getPvalue(object, test, name=NULL)
1066	    res <- cbind(res[c("baseMean","log2FoldChange","lfcSE")],stat,pvalue)
1067	    names(res) <- c("baseMean","log2FoldChange","lfcSE","stat","pvalue")
1068	  }
1069	  
1070	  return(res)
1071	}

2352	
2353	calculateCooksDistance <- function(object, H, modelMatrix) {
2354	  p <- ncol(modelMatrix)
2355	  dispersions <- robustMethodOfMomentsDisp(object, modelMatrix)
2356	  V <- assays(object)[["mu"]] + dispersions * assays(object)[["mu"]]^2
2357	  PearsonResSq <- (counts(object) - assays(object)[["mu"]])^2 / V
2358	  cooks <- PearsonResSq / p  * H / (1 - H)^2
2359	  cooks
2360	}
2361	
2362

788	# c' beta / sqrt( c' sigma c)
789	# where beta is the coefficient vector
790	# and sigma is the covariance matrix for beta
791	getContrast <- function(object, contrast, useT=FALSE, minmu) {
792	  if (missing(contrast)) {
793	    stop("must provide a contrast")
794	  }
795	  if (is.null(attr(object,"modelMatrix"))) {
796	    stop("was expecting a model matrix stored as an attribute of the DESeqDataSet")
797	  }
798	  modelMatrix <- attr(object, "modelMatrix")
799	  
800	  # only continue on the rows with non-zero row mean
801	  objectNZ <- object[!mcols(object)$allZero,]
802	  normalizationFactors <- getSizeOrNormFactors(objectNZ)
803	  alpha_hat <- dispersions(objectNZ)
804	  coefColumns <- names(mcols(objectNZ))[grep("log2 fold change",mcols(mcols(object))$description)]
805	  # convert betas to log scale
806	  beta_mat <- log(2) * as.matrix(mcols(objectNZ)[,coefColumns,drop=FALSE])
807	  # convert beta prior variance to log scale
808	  lambda = 1/(log(2)^2 * attr(object,"betaPriorVar"))
809	
810	  # check if DESeq() replaced outliers
811	  countsMatrix <- if ("replaceCounts" %in% assayNames(object)) {
812	    assays(objectNZ)[["replaceCounts"]]
813	  } else {
814	    counts(objectNZ)
815	  }
816	
817	  # use weights if they are present in assays(object)
818	  if ("weights" %in% assayNames(object)) {
819	    useWeights <- TRUE
820	    weights <- assays(objectNZ)[["weights"]]
821	    stopifnot(all(weights >= 0))
822	    weights <- weights / apply(weights, 1, max)
823	  } else {
824	    useWeights <- FALSE
825	    weights <- matrix(1, nrow=nrow(object), ncol=ncol(object))
826	  }
827	  
828	  betaRes <- fitBeta(ySEXP = countsMatrix, xSEXP = modelMatrix,
829	                     nfSEXP = normalizationFactors,
830	                     alpha_hatSEXP = alpha_hat,
831	                     contrastSEXP = contrast,
832	                     beta_matSEXP = beta_mat,
833	                     lambdaSEXP = lambda,
834	                     weightsSEXP = weights,
835	                     useWeightsSEXP = useWeights,
836	                     tolSEXP = 1e-8, maxitSEXP = 0,
837	                     useQRSEXP=FALSE, # QR not relevant, fitting loop isn't entered
838	                     minmuSEXP=minmu)
839	  # convert back to log2 scale
840	  contrastEstimate <- log2(exp(1)) * betaRes$contrast_num
841	  contrastSE <- log2(exp(1)) * betaRes$contrast_denom
842	  contrastStatistic <- contrastEstimate / contrastSE
843	  if (useT) {
844	    stopifnot("tDegreesFreedom" %in% names(mcols(object)))
845	    df <- mcols(objectNZ)$tDegreesFreedom
846	    contrastPvalue <- 2*pt(abs(contrastStatistic),df=df,lower.tail=FALSE)
847	  } else {
848	    contrastPvalue <- 2*pnorm(abs(contrastStatistic),lower.tail=FALSE)
849	  }
850	  contrastList <- list(log2FoldChange=contrastEstimate,
851	                       lfcSE=contrastSE,
852	                       stat=contrastStatistic,
853	                       pvalue=contrastPvalue)
854	  contrastResults <- buildDataFrameWithNARows(contrastList,
855	                                              mcols(object)$allZero)
856	  names(contrastResults) <- c("log2FoldChange","lfcSE","stat","pvalue")
857	  contrastResults
858	}

860	# this function takes a desired contrast as specified by results(),
861	# performs checks, and then either returns the already existing contrast
862	# or generates the contrast by calling getContrast() using a numeric vector
863	cleanContrast <- function(object, contrast, expanded=FALSE, listValues, test, useT, minmu) {
864	  # get the names of columns in the beta matrix
865	  resNames <- resultsNames(object)
866	  # if possible, return pre-computed columns, which are
867	  # already stored in mcols(dds). this will be the case using
868	  # results() with 'name', or if expanded model matrices were not
869	  # run and the contrast contains the reference level as numerator or denominator
870	
871	  resReady <- FALSE
872	  
873	  if (is.character(contrast)) {
874	    contrastFactor <- contrast[1]
875	    if (!contrastFactor %in% names(colData(object))) {
876	      stop(paste(contrastFactor,"should be the name of a factor in the colData of the DESeqDataSet"))
877	    }
878	    if (!is(colData(object)[[contrastFactor]], "factor")) {
879	      stop(paste(contrastFactor,"is not a factor, see ?results"))
880	    }
881	    contrastNumLevel <- contrast[2]
882	    contrastDenomLevel <- contrast[3]
883	    contrastBaseLevel <- levels(colData(object)[,contrastFactor])[1]
884	    
885	    # check for intercept
886	    hasIntercept <- attr(terms(design(object)),"intercept") == 1
887	    firstVar <- contrastFactor == all.vars(design(object))[1]
888	
889	    # tricky case: if the design has no intercept, the factor is
890	    # not the first variable in the design, and one of the numerator or denominator
891	    # is the reference level, then the desired contrast is simply a coefficient (or -1 times)
892	    noInterceptPullCoef <- !hasIntercept & !firstVar &
893	      (contrastBaseLevel %in% c(contrastNumLevel, contrastDenomLevel))
894	    
895	    # case 1: standard model matrices: pull coef or build the appropriate contrast
896	    # coefficients names are of the form  "factor_level_vs_baselevel"
897	    # output: contrastNumColumn and contrastDenomColumn
898	    if (!expanded & (hasIntercept | noInterceptPullCoef)) {
899	      # use make.names() so the column names are
900	      # the same as created by DataFrame in mcols(object).
901	      contrastNumColumn <- make.names(paste0(contrastFactor,"_",contrastNumLevel,"_vs_",contrastBaseLevel))
902	      contrastDenomColumn <- make.names(paste0(contrastFactor,"_",contrastDenomLevel,"_vs_",contrastBaseLevel))
903	      # check that the desired contrast is already
904	      # available in mcols(object), and then we can either
905	      # take it directly or multiply the log fold
906	      # changes and Wald stat by -1
907	      if ( contrastDenomLevel == contrastBaseLevel ) {
908	        cleanName <- paste(contrastFactor,contrastNumLevel,"vs",contrastDenomLevel)
909	        # the results can be pulled directly from mcols(object)
910	        name <- if (!noInterceptPullCoef) {
911	          make.names(paste0(contrastFactor,"_",contrastNumLevel,"_vs_",contrastDenomLevel))
912	        } else {
913	          make.names(paste0(contrastFactor,contrastNumLevel))
914	        }
915	        if (!name %in% resNames) {
916	          stop(paste("as",contrastDenomLevel,"is the reference level, was expecting",name,"to be present in 'resultsNames(object)'"))
917	        }
918	        log2FoldChange <- getCoef(object, name)
919	        lfcSE <- getCoefSE(object, name)
920	        stat <- getStat(object, test, name)
921	        pvalue <- getPvalue(object, test, name)
922	        res <- cbind(mcols(object)["baseMean"],log2FoldChange,lfcSE,stat,pvalue)
923	        names(res) <- c("baseMean","log2FoldChange","lfcSE","stat","pvalue")
924	        lfcType <- if (attr(object,"betaPrior")) "MAP" else "MLE"
925	        lfcDesc <- paste0("log2 fold change (",lfcType,"): ",cleanName)
926	        mcols(res,use.names=TRUE)["log2FoldChange","description"] <- lfcDesc
927	        resReady <- TRUE
928	        
929	      } else if ( contrastNumLevel == contrastBaseLevel ) {
930	        # fetch the results for denom vs num 
931	        # and mutiply the log fold change and stat by -1
932	        cleanName <- paste(contrastFactor,contrastNumLevel,"vs",contrastDenomLevel)
933	        swapName <- if (!noInterceptPullCoef) {
934	          make.names(paste0(contrastFactor,"_",contrastDenomLevel,"_vs_",contrastNumLevel))
935	        } else {
936	          make.names(paste0(contrastFactor,contrastDenomLevel))
937	        }
938	        if (!swapName %in% resNames) {
939	          stop(paste("as",contrastNumLevel,"is the reference level, was expecting",swapName,"to be present in 'resultsNames(object)'"))
940	        }
941	        log2FoldChange <- getCoef(object, swapName)
942	        lfcSE <- getCoefSE(object, swapName)
943	        stat <- getStat(object, test, swapName)
944	        pvalue <- getPvalue(object, test, swapName)
945	        res <- cbind(mcols(object)["baseMean"],log2FoldChange,lfcSE,stat,pvalue)
946	        names(res) <- c("baseMean","log2FoldChange","lfcSE","stat","pvalue")
947	        res$log2FoldChange <- -1 * res$log2FoldChange
948	        if (test == "Wald") res$stat <- -1 * res$stat
949	        lfcType <- if (attr(object,"betaPrior")) "MAP" else "MLE"
950	        # rename some of the columns using the flipped contrast
951	        if (test == "Wald") {
952	          contrastDescriptions <- paste(c(paste0("log2 fold change (",lfcType,"):"),
953	                                          "standard error:",
954	                                          "Wald statistic:","Wald test p-value:"), cleanName)
955	          mcols(res,use.names=TRUE)[c("log2FoldChange","lfcSE","stat","pvalue"),
956	                      "description"] <- contrastDescriptions
957	        } else {
958	          contrastDescriptions <- paste(c(paste0("log2 fold change (",lfcType,"):"),
959	                                          "standard error:"), cleanName)
960	          mcols(res,use.names=TRUE)[c("log2FoldChange","lfcSE"),
961	                      "description"] <- contrastDescriptions
962	        }
963	        resReady <- TRUE
964	        
965	      } else {
966	        # check for the case where neither are present
967	        # as comparisons against reference level
968	        if ( ! (contrastNumColumn %in% resNames &
969	                  contrastDenomColumn %in% resNames) ) {
970	          stop(paste(contrastNumLevel,"and",contrastDenomLevel,"should be levels of",contrastFactor,"such that",contrastNumColumn,"and",contrastDenomColumn,"are contained in 'resultsNames(object)'"))
971	        }
972	      }
973	      # case 2: expanded model matrices or no intercept and first variable
974	      # need to then build the appropriate contrast.
975	      # these coefficient names have the form "factorlevel"
976	      # output: contrastNumColumn and contrastDenomColumn
977	    } else {
978	      # we only need to check validity
979	      contrastNumColumn <- make.names(paste0(contrastFactor, contrastNumLevel))
980	      contrastDenomColumn <- make.names(paste0(contrastFactor, contrastDenomLevel))
981	      if ( ! (contrastNumColumn %in% resNames & contrastDenomColumn %in% resNames) ) {
982	        stop(paste(paste0(contrastFactor,contrastNumLevel),"and",paste0(contrastFactor,contrastDenomLevel),
983	                   "are expected to be in resultsNames(object)"))
984	      }
985	    }
986	
987	    # check if both levels have all zero counts
988	    # (this has to be down here to make use of error checking above)
989	    contrastAllZero <- contrastAllZeroCharacter(object, contrastFactor,
990	                                                contrastNumLevel, contrastDenomLevel)
991	    
992	  }
993	
994	  # if the result table not already built in the above code
995	  if (!resReady) {
996	    
997	    # here, a numeric / list / character contrast which will be converted

1014	        listvalname2 <- if (listvalname2 == 1) "" else paste0(listvalname2," ")

1016	      } else if (lc1 > 0 & lc2 == 0) {

nbinomWaldTest <- function(object,
                           betaPrior=FALSE, betaPriorVar,
                           modelMatrix=NULL, modelMatrixType,
                           betaTol=1e-8, maxit=100, useOptim=TRUE, quiet=FALSE,
                           useT=FALSE, df, useQR=TRUE, minmu=0.5) {
  if (is.null(dispersions(object))) {
    stop("testing requires dispersion estimates, first call estimateDispersions()")
  }
  stopifnot(length(maxit)==1)
  # in case the class of the mcols(mcols(object)) are not character
  object <- sanitizeRowRanges(object)

  # integer check
  if ( !is.integer( counts(object) ) ) {
    stop( "the count data is not in integer mode and fitType is not 'glmGamPoi'" )
